description_long: This is a classic double-decker bus, reminiscent of the iconic London Routemaster, featuring two levels of seating visible through large rectangular windows on both sides. The bus has a boxy, elongated body with rounded roof edges and a distinct open entry platform at the rear, which is typical for vintage public transport vehicles. The lower deck has a front cab with a prominent wheel arch and a separate entry area, while the upper deck is accessible via an internal staircase. The windows are evenly spaced, with each deck having a row of large, rectangular panes separated by thin pillars. The bus sits on four large wheels, with the front wheels slightly inset under the cab and the rear wheels positioned near the back. The exterior is smooth and streamlined, with no visible signage, logos, or route indicators.
description: A vintage double-decker bus with two rows of large rectangular windows, an open rear entry platform, and a smooth, rounded roof.
description_view_0: Side view showing both decks, large rectangular windows, and the open rear entry platform.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the cab, front windows, and the entry platform on the left side.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with similar window arrangement and visible front cab details.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back windows, rear wheels, and the open entry platform.
attribute_window_arrangement: Two rows of large, evenly spaced rectangular windows on each side, separated by thin vertical pillars; upper and lower decks have similar window patterns.
